name: Brazil
summary: Brazil's commodity complex and a rapidly digitizing financial sector dominate local headlines. Fiscal policy signals and Petrobras dividend politics move markets weeks before global desks react.
economicDevelopments: Central bank (Copom) holding rates as disinflation stalls; market debating cut timing.
Record soybean and iron-ore export volumes lifting trade surplus.
Real-denominated corporate debt issuance reopening after a quiet quarter.
regulatoryDevelopments: ANP reviewing offshore pre-salt licensing terms — potential margin impact for operators.
Central bank advancing Drex (digital real) pilot with major banks and fintechs.
infrastructure: BR-163 logistics corridor expansion to cut grain freight costs from Mato Grosso.
Porto do Açu green-hydrogen and ammonia export terminal reaching FID stage.

name: India
summary: The fastest-growing major economy. Capex announcements, PLI scheme expansions, and IT-services demand commentary frequently surface in regional-language and trade press first.
economicDevelopments: Government front-loading infrastructure capex ahead of state elections.
Services PMI at multi-year highs; manufacturing PLI incentives broadening.
Strong domestic SIP inflows underpinning equity-market liquidity.
regulatoryDevelopments: SEBI tightening derivatives margin and disclosure norms for retail flow.
New semiconductor and display-fab incentive tranche under cabinet review.
infrastructure: Dedicated Freight Corridor commissioning new sections, cutting logistics time.
Green-energy corridor transmission build-out to evacuate Gujarat/Rajasthan solar.

name: Mexico
summary: Nearshoring is the defining theme — industrial-park leasing, border logistics, and USMCA content rules drive corporate winners. Local business dailies break expansion news early.
economicDevelopments: Nearshoring driving record FDI into Bajío and northern industrial corridors.
Banxico signaling a measured easing path as the peso stays resilient.
Industrial real-estate vacancy near record lows in border markets.
regulatoryDevelopments: Energy-sector permitting reforms creating uncertainty for private generators.
USMCA auto rules-of-origin enforcement reshaping supplier sourcing.
infrastructure: Interoceanic Corridor (Tehuantepec) rail and port upgrades progressing.
Cross-border power interconnection studies to serve new manufacturing load.

name: Indonesia
summary: The nickel and EV-battery supply chain story sits at the center of Indonesian markets, alongside a fast-growing digital-banking sector. Downstreaming policy shifts originate locally.
economicDevelopments: Resource downstreaming policy boosting domestic nickel processing value-add.
Digital banks scaling deposits rapidly among younger urban customers.
New-capital (Nusantara) spending supporting construction-materials demand.
regulatoryDevelopments: Export-ban expansion to additional raw minerals under discussion.
Bank Indonesia macroprudential tweaks to manage rupiah stability.
infrastructure: Nusantara (IKN) new-capital build-out entering major construction phase.
Battery-grade nickel HPAL projects ramping in Sulawesi.

name: Vietnam
summary: A core 'China+1' manufacturing beneficiary. Electronics FDI, real-estate liquidity, and an awaited emerging-market index reclassification are the stories that move local indices.
economicDevelopments: Electronics and apparel export orders recovering on inventory restocking.
Property-sector liquidity measures aimed at stabilizing developers.
Potential MSCI/FTSE emerging-market reclassification in focus.
regulatoryDevelopments: Securities-market upgrades to meet index-reclassification settlement rules.
Power Development Plan VIII reshaping renewable and LNG investment.
infrastructure: Long Thanh International Airport construction advancing near Ho Chi Minh City.
North-South expressway and industrial-zone power upgrades underway.

name: South Korea
summary: The global memory and HBM (high-bandwidth memory) cycle is set here. Korean tech and battery makers' supplier commentary is a leading indicator for the AI hardware complex worldwide.
economicDevelopments: Memory pricing inflecting upward on AI-driven HBM demand.
Exports rebounding led by semiconductors and autos.
'Value-up' corporate-governance program targeting equity re-rating.
regulatoryDevelopments: Corporate 'value-up' disclosure guidelines to address the 'Korea discount'.
Chip-fab tax-credit extension supporting capex commitments.
infrastructure: Yongin mega semiconductor cluster site preparation and power planning.
Battery gigafactory supply agreements with global automakers expanding.

name: Turkey
summary: A high-stakes orthodox-policy normalization story. Rate decisions, reserve rebuilding, and a defense/industrial exports boom dominate — local financial press leads global coverage.
economicDevelopments: Orthodox monetary tightening aimed at taming inflation and rebuilding reserves.
Defense and machinery exports expanding to new markets.
Foreign portfolio inflows returning as real rates turn positive.
regulatoryDevelopments: Simplification of macroprudential rules to normalize the banking system.
Capital-markets incentives to deepen local institutional participation.
infrastructure: Gas-discovery development in the Black Sea (Sakarya field) ramping output.
Renewable-capacity auctions to reduce energy-import dependence.
